source: generated full-random (seed 14123330213291275571, seed_index 0, max-boundaries 384) DST trace at generator lash-sim.generated-workload.v8 on 2026-06-29; surfaced by the broad/full generated lane
classification: RESOLVED. This was NOT a product bug. The apparent cross-backend divergence was a replay-FIDELITY gap in the harness's own SQLite re-drive: the old `replay-sqlite` path re-drove a recorded trace in fixed order with provider exchanges GATED to the original in-memory run's recorded (exchange_index,event_index) counts, which deadlocked on the active-turn queued-input enqueue and could surface an extra exchange / empty output. Driven directly through one driver, the real in-memory store and the real lash-sqlite-store commit identical output.
verdict_evidence: The backend-equivalence test crates/lash-sim/tests/cross_backend_active_turn_divergence.rs builds two real lash::StandardCore instances (in-memory vs lash-sqlite-store) over an UN-GATED scripted transport and proves they agree across the active-turn enqueue / cancel / claim / complete orderings (cancel-before-turn, cancel-after-turn, no-cancel-claim, claim-then-cancel).
resolution: The cross-backend lane no longer uses a separate gated, fixed-exchange-count serial re-drive. It now re-runs the SAME generated workload through the SAME scheduler-driven, concurrency-faithful driver (run_generated_workload / drive_generated_workload), parameterized only by the store factory (replay_workload_on_sqlite in crates/lash-sim/src/runner.rs), and compares the resulting abstract world summary against the in-memory reference. Apples-to-apples by construction, so the fidelity gap cannot exist.
model_replay: passes (this trace is model-replayable; the trace and abstract model were always sound)
minimization: not applied — the former fidelity gap was scale-dependent (it only appeared in the gated re-drive at broad/full scale). The full broad-lane trace is retained as the canonical fixture pinning the active-turn-cancel shape.
scale_evidence: 491 events, 4 sessions, each with an active-turn queued-input cancel followed by subsequent provider turns (e.g. session-003 has 7 provider outputs after its cancel).
guard: crates/lash-sim/src/replay.rs::cross_backend_active_turn_fixture_model_replays_and_pins_shape keeps this trace model-replayable and pins the shape under `cargo test`.
